generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts desired speed commands from Digital Twin to motor voltage commands for the physical vehicle's propulsion system"
]

instance SteeringServoController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls steering servo based on ACC actuation commands from Digital Twin, ensuring precise lane-keeping and adaptive steering"
]

instance EmergencyBrakeActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Implements emergency braking commands with 100ms latency for safety-critical ACC operations, interfacing with vehicle braking system"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR providing 2D plane coordinates for object detection and lead vehicle tracking in vehicle's proximity"
]

instance DepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel Deepsense D435i Depth Camera capturing environmental context for perception and object recognition"
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects motor, steering, servo actuation, and general vehicle metrics for real-time state monitoring"
]

// C4: Data Transmission Components
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
    base:desc "Raw 2D plane coordinates from LiDAR processed through Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking for object tracks and environmental maps"
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    base:desc "Normalized vehicle state data (speed, position, sensor readings) for real-time synchronization with Digital Twin"
]

instance ObjectDetectionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
    base:desc "Processed object tracks from LiDAR for proactive lead vehicle tracking in ACC system"
]

instance ActuationCommands : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleSpeedController, SteeringServoController
    base:desc "Desired speed and steering commands generated by Digital Twin for remote actuation in ACC system"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ACCPerformanceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model capturing ACC system dynamics including lead vehicle detection, speed regulation algorithms, and safety constraints"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalMapModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric representation of driving environment derived from LiDAR point cloud data for spatial awareness"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataPreProcessingEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance StateDataLog : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured log of vehicle state data for offline analysis, performance reporting, and developer validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReportingEnabler, DatabaseStorageEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Database storing processed object tracks and detection history for proactive tracking analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectDetectionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationEnabler
]

instance PerformanceReport : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Generated performance metrics and analytics exported for offline analysis by developers"
    DTDFVocab:fromData StateDataLog, ObjectTrackDatabase
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ReportingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time communication framework (Robot Operating System 2) enabling modular software component communication between physical and digital entities"
]

instance DataPreProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity performing data normalization, unit conversions (e.g., m/s to km/h), and alignment with Digital Twin models"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARPointCloudData, VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationFE, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ISO 23247-compliant Functional Entity standardizing message types and ensuring compatibility across heterogeneous data streams"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARPointCloudData, VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:enables ACCPerformanceModel, EnvironmentalMapModel
]

instance CloudComputeEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Remote compute infrastructure offloading ACC-specific computations (lead-vehicle detection, speed calculation) from resource-constrained ECUs"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCPerformanceModel
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Presentation Functional Entity providing real-time visualization of vehicle state data and object tracks in virtual cockpit"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnvironmentalMapModel
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ReportingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity handling automated logging, performance report generation, and data export for offline analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateDataLog, PerformanceReport
    DTDFVocab:enables DataLoggingService
]

instance DatabaseStorageEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database implementation for structured data storage of vehicle state logs and performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateDataLog
    DTDFVocab:enables DataPersistenceService
]

instance WiFiUDPTransport : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "UDP-based Wi-Fi communication layer enabling cross-vehicle data exchange between teleoperated vehicle and Digital Twin"
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of vehicle state data (speed, position, sensor readings) via user interface with latency-sensitive synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeVisualizationInsight, DataLoggingAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC computations (lead-vehicle detection, speed calculation) to cloud-based infrastructure for scalability"
    DTDFVocab:provides DesiredSpeedCalculationAction, PerformanceBenchmarkingInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Operator-facing UI enabling remote control of ACC systems with 100ms latency for emergency braking commands"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCStateManagementAction, CustomSpeedSettingAction, EmergencyBrakeTriggerAction
]

instance DeveloperSupportService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Developer-facing capabilities for troubleshooting including real-time diagnostics, data collection, and performance benchmarking"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataCollectionAction, AlgorithmValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D vehicle model visualization in virtual cockpit showing real-time speed, position, and object tracks with projected driving paths"
]

instance DataLoggingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated saving of vehicle state data logs for offline analysis and performance reporting"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DesiredSpeedCalculationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Digital Twin computes desired speed based on distance to lead vehicle and forwards command to physical vehicle for actuation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceBenchmarkingInsight : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Comparative analysis of ACC system performance metrics across different driving conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ACCStateManagementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enable/disable ACC mode dynamically through operator interface with immediate system state updates"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance CustomSpeedSettingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator sets custom desired speeds for specific traffic conditions via remote management interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeTriggerAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Immediate emergency braking command with 100ms latency for safety-critical scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataCollectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Developer-triggered data collection for algorithm validation and performance testing"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AlgorithmValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results comparing ACC algorithms' performance against real-world driving conditions"
]

instance DataPersistenceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling data persistence operations for structured storage"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataPersistenceAction
]

instance DataPersistenceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for storing data in database"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance VehicleAutonomyEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiDARSensor, DepthCamera, VehicleStateSensor,
        VehicleSpeedController, SteeringServoController,
        EmergencyBrakeActuator, ROS2Middleware
]

instance DigitalTwinCoreEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ACCPerformanceModel, EnvironmentalMapModel,
        DataPreProcessingEnabler, DataTranslationFE,
        CloudComputeEnabler, VisualizationEnabler,
        ReportingEnabler, DatabaseStorageEnabler
]

instance OperatorInterfaceEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ROS2Middleware, WiFiUDPTransport
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  **Not in Document**
  *(No explicit details provided about control flows from digital to physical, including command types, validation, or feedback systems.)*
 "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system enforces **strict time synchronization** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and real-time coordination. The system mandates **clock synchronization accuracy of ≤100 ms** for safety-critical vehicular applications, driven by hardware limitations and the need for precise temporal alignment across distributed entities. This requirement ensures deterministic timing for critical operations such as sensor data processing, actuation commands, and data pre-processing, where even minor delays could compromise system safety or performance. The temporal granularity is aligned with the operational cadence of automotive systems, where millisecond-level precision is often essential for tasks like point cloud clustering and tracking within the Physical Twin Entity.
  "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin system follows an **entity-centric architecture** rooted in ISO 23247 RA, with three core entities mapped to hardware components:
    - **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts compute, sensors, actuators, and general automotive functions (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) via ROS2 packages.
    - **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data pre-processing, actuation, and control mechanisms, diverging from ISO 23247 by encapsulating broader system resources.
    - **User Entity**: Contains the singular _User Interface FE_, split into **3D Visualization** and **Control Panel** components, executed as ROS2 nodes.
    The orchestration relies on **ROS2’s modularity**, where each FE is implemented as an independent package, enabling dynamic integration and scalability. **Component integration** is facilitated via ROS2’s topic/service model, ensuring inter-entity communication. Resource management is implicit in the entity-based deployment, where hardware ownership aligns with functional ownership. The system’s **system-wide coordination** is implicit in the ISO 23247 RA alignment, though deviations (e.g., expanded Physical Twin Entity scope) introduce customization trade-offs.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The system emphasizes **high-fidelity digital representation** of OMEs, ensuring data integrity via **clock synchronization** for real-time data exchange. While the ISO 23247 framework mandates **data integrity**, validation and verification methods, uncertainty quantification, and error correction mechanisms are **not explicitly detailed** in the documents. The Digital Twin Entity’s fidelity is implicitly tied to the accuracy of the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME) and its digital twin alignment, but no specific methodologies (e.g., ISO/IEC 15288) or quality assurance processes are referenced.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Technical Network Infrastructure:**
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - **Intra-entity (within Digital Twin):** ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) for software component communication.
    - **Inter-entity (physical ↔ digital):** Wi-Fi over **UDP** (User Datagram Protocol) in the transport layer.

  - **Network Topology & Architecture:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Localized, likely on the vehicle’s onboard system (edge computing).
    - **Wi-Fi UDP:** Connects physical and digital twins over a **broadband Wi-Fi network** (e.g., cloud/edge gateway).

  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements:**
    - **Real-time criticality:** LiDAR data (point clouds) and vehicle state updates require **low latency** (likely <100ms for steering/motor commands).
    - **Bandwidth:** Not specified, but high-throughput required for LiDAR (e.g., 100+ Mbps for point cloud streams).

  - **Reliability and Redundancy:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Built-in fault tolerance (e.g., message retries, deadlock handling).
    - **Wi-Fi UDP:** No explicit redundancy, but UDP’s packet loss handling may mitigate interruptions.
    - **No mention of hardware redundancy** (e.g., dual LiDAR sensors) or failover mechanisms.

  - **Security Protocols:**
    - **Data Translation FE:** Ensures **unit conversions and message alignment**, but **no explicit security** (e.g., encryption, authentication) is described.
    - **ROS2 Network:** May use ROS security plugins (e.g., TLS for message encryption), but not detailed.
    - **Wi-Fi UDP:** Vulnerable to eavesdropping; **no mention of encryption** (e.g., WPA3, TLS) for inter-entity communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  **Deployment Infrastructure:**
  - **Hosting Platforms:**
    - **Edge Computing:** Primary deployment (e.g., onboard vehicle systems for real-time processing).
    - **Cloud/On-Premise:** Likely used for **data storage, reporting, and visualization** (e.g., 3D cockpit).
    - **No explicit mention of hybrid cloud** or distributed architectures.

  - **Computational and Storage Requirements:**
    - **Real-time Processing:**
      - **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking:** Requires **high-performance CPUs/GPUs** (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson for LiDAR processing).
      - **ROS2 Nodes:** Lightweight but scalable for multiple sensors (e.g., motor/steering actuation).
    - **Storage:**
      - **Data Storage FE:** Handles raw/processed data (e.g., LiDAR logs, vehicle telemetry).
      - **Reporting FE:** Generates summaries (e.g., driving paths, diagnostics) for long-term storage.

  - **Scalability Mechanisms:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Supports **horizontal scaling** (e.g., adding more nodes for sensor fusion).
    - **Edge-Cloud Hybrid:** Offloads heavy computations (e.g., 3D visualization) to cloud if needed.
    - **No explicit mention of auto-scaling** (e.g., Kubernetes) or dynamic resource allocation.

  - **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - **Onboard Vehicle (Edge):** Primary for real-time control (e.g., motor/steering actuation).
    - **Cloud-Based:** For **reporting, visualization, and historical data analysis**.
    - **No mention of IoT gateways** or microservices architectures.

  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance:**
    - **ROS2 Network:** Built-in resilience (e.g., message retries, deadlock prevention).
    - **Wi-Fi UDP:** No redundancy; **no SLA guarantees** for inter-entity communication.
    - **No explicit redundancy** (e.g., dual power supplies, failover switches) is described.
    - **Availability:** Implied to be **high** for onboard systems but **not quantified** (e.g., 99.9% uptime).
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
    Not in Document
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard (Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing) is referenced, specifically Part 2: Reference Architecture, which defines four core entities:
  1. **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**: Represents physical assets (e.g., equipment, facilities, processes) with observable attributes.
  2. **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Manages data acquisition from OMEs and controls their operations.
  3. **Digital Twin Entity**: Provides a digital replica of the OME, including its state, behavior, and historical data.
  4. **User Entity**: Facilitates human interaction via interfaces (e.g., monitoring dashboards, control panels).

  The standard does not mandate specific technologies but suggests **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** as an example protocol for Functional Entities (FEs) to enable interoperability between entities. No additional standards (e.g., certification frameworks, compliance frameworks) or interoperability specifications are mentioned in the provided documents."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system must adhere to the performance and operational requirements of **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)** as specified in **ISO 15622:2018**, ensuring compliance with its test procedures for system accuracy, latency, and reliability. For **Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication**, the system must implement the **3GPP TS 22.185: Release 15** service requirements, which define the technical framework for **Vehicle-to-Vehicle (V2V)**, **Vehicle-to-Infrastructure (V2I)**, and **Vehicle-to-Network (V2N)** interactions, including secure data exchange frameworks.

The system prioritizes **safety-critical operations**, aligning with the functional safety standards of the automotive domain. While no explicit mention of **ISO 26262-1:2018** (Functional Safety for Road Vehicles) is present, the underlying architecture implies adherence to **ASIL (Automotive Safety Integrity Level) requirements** for critical control functions, ensuring fail-safe mechanisms and risk mitigation for autonomous driving scenarios. Access control mechanisms are implied through the structured communication protocols of V2X services, though no specific authentication systems (e.g., digital signatures, PKI) or threat mitigation strategies (e.g., intrusion detection) are detailed in the documents."
]
